EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A synoptic pattern with high pressure ridging to the east of Nevada and an upper low to the northwest created a strong southwesterly flow over the silver state. This pattern brought some high winds, moisture, and some light precipitation across the region.
Atmospheric stability above the Ruby Mountains overnight produced a mountain downslope effect into the Ruby Valley that created strong gusty winds.

A strong winter storm impacted the region from the 3rd to the 5th of January. This produced between 1 to 2 feet of snow in the Tahoe Basin with 2 to 4 feet for the higher elevations. This system also produced winds upwards of 90 mph on the ridges, with gusts commonly upwards of 55 mph elsewhere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mount Rose Ski Base reported 25 inches of snow, trained spotter at 1 NNW Incline Village measured 19.5 inches with a SWE of 2.71 inches, and Diamond Peak Ski Base reported 12 inches. These are storm totals.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: This storm was followed by a quick-moving cold front during the night of the 7th and early morning of the 8th, which produced a burst of snow across lower elevations of western NV. Most snowfall amounts were light (1-4���) but roads became icy with significant travel impacts due to temperatures dropping well below freezing. This system also produced the first and only measurable snow (1.5���) of the winter season so far at the Reno Airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: CoCoHaRs station NV-ST-2 3 NNW Virginia City measured 5 inches, CoCoRaHs station NV-WH-79 17.8 NNW measured 4 inches. Trained spotter 3 NE Spanish Springs measured 3 inches with a SWE of 0.15 inches.
